title: Comparatives & Superlatives
description: Compare things in English: bigger than, more expensive than, and the biggest of all.

cards.notes: Short adjective: big → **bigger** (double the g, add -er).
Joins the two things compared: *bigger **than** that*.
Superlative of long adjectives: **the most** + adjective.

cards.front: This house is bigger than that one.
cards.back: यह घर उस घर से बड़ा है।

steps.title: Comparing two things
steps.body:
# Comparatives: comparing two things

**Short adjectives** (1 syllable): add **-er** + **than**.

- big → **bigger than**  ·  cheap → **cheaper than**  ·  old → **older than**

**Long adjectives** (2+ syllables): use **more** + adjective + **than**.

- expensive → **more expensive than**  ·  beautiful → **more beautiful than**

**Irregular:** good → **better**, bad → **worse**.

> *This house is **bigger than** that one.* (यह घर उससे बड़ा है।)

The word **than** (से) connects the two things you compare.

steps.title: The most of all (superlatives)
steps.body:
# Superlatives: the most of all

Use **the** with the superlative when one thing is top of the group.

**Short adjectives:** the + adjective + **-est**

- big → **the biggest**  ·  cheap → **the cheapest**

**Long adjectives:** **the most** + adjective

- expensive → **the most expensive**  ·  beautiful → **the most beautiful**

**Irregular:** good → **the best**, bad → **the worst**.

> *This is **the biggest** house in the village.* (सबसे बड़ा घर)

Don't forget **the** before a superlative.

exercise.prompt: Match each English form with its Hindi meaning.
Complete: "This house is bigger ___ that one."
exercise.sentence: This house is bigger ___ that one.
exercise.hint: The word that joins two compared things (से).

exercise.prompt: Complete: "Mount Everest is the ___ mountain in the world." (high)
exercise.sentence: Mount Everest is the ___ mountain in the world.

exercise.prompt: Make the comparative of "expensive" (a long adjective).
exercise.hint: Long adjectives use a word before them, not -er.

exercise.prompt: Arrange to mean: ट्रेन बस से तेज़ है। (The train is faster than the bus.)
